searchDocs.title: Setting SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: SoundGroups Are Automatically determined in the Sounds Folder, There is no need to manually set it yourself.  The First Set of children in the Sound folder must be Configs/Folders as the name of that folder is used to determine the sound group. And Sounds under the sub-folder is automatically grouped.

searchDocs.title: Wally Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Once Wally is installed, run wally init on your project directory, and then add VluxySF as a dependency. For example, the following could be a wally.toml file for a project that includes a few of these modules:  [package] name = "your_name/your_project" version = "0.1.0" registry = "https://github.com/UpliftGames/wally-index" realm = "shared" [dependencies] VluxySF = "greenviper126/VluxySF@^1"   To install this dependency, run wally install within your project. Wally will create a Package folder in your directory with the installed dependency.

searchDocs.title: Rojo Configuration​
searchDocs.content: The Package folder created by Wally should be synced into Roblox Studio through your Rojo configuration. For instance, a Rojo configuration might have the following entry to sync the Packages folder into ReplicatedStorage:  { "name": "vluxysf", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "$className": "ReplicatedStorage", "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: Setup​
searchDocs.content: In order to start using VluxySF you need to initialize the package on both the server and client.

searchDocs.title: SoundFolder​
searchDocs.content: In order to setup the soundFolder see this page Configuring Sounds

searchDocs.title: Server​
searchDocs.content: On the server you are required to inject the config/folder location of all your instanced sounds. This Folder should be located on the server.  local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) --folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use. local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") --initialize the server VluxySF._initServer(soundFolder)

searchDocs.title: Client​
searchDocs.content: local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) --initialize the client VluxySF._initClient()

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinition = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound effect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundEffectDefinition> Defines the sound effects of a sound as a dictionary of class names to their corresponding properties.

searchDocs.title: SoundProperties​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundProperties = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinition​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundDefinition { Properties: SoundProperties-- The properties of the sound itself, such as SoundId, Volume, etc. Effects: SoundEffectDefinitions-- The sound effects of the sound, organized by class name. } Defines the properties and sound effects of a sound.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundDefinition> Defines the sound definitions of the system as a dictionary of sound names to their corresponding sound data.

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundGroups = Dictionary<SoundGroup > Defines the type of a sound group instance.

searchDocs.title: SoundVariance​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundVariance { PlaybackSpeed: () → number-- A callback that returns a multiplier for the sound's playback speed. Volume: () → number-- A callback that returns a multiplier for the sound's volume. } Defines the variance for sound properties, allowing for dynamic changes to playback speed and volume.

searchDocs.title: SoundSchema​
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundSchema { UnattachedSounds: Configuration -- A folder for storing unattached sounds. SoundGroupContainer: Configuration -- A folder for storing sound groups. SoundGroups: SoundGroups-- A dictionary of sound group instances, organized by name. SoundDefinitions: SoundDefinitions-- A dictionary of sound definitions, organized by sound name. } Defines the overall schema for the sound system, containing all necessary data for sound creation and management.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.SoundDefinitions() → SoundDefinitions-- All converted sound data Returns all serialized sound data.

searchDocs.title: AllSoundNames​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.AllSoundNames() → {string}-- All registered sound names Returns all sound names.

searchDocs.title: FindSoundGroup​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.FindSoundGroup( name: string-- Name of the sound group ) → SoundGroup? -- Matching sound group, if found Finds a sound group by name.

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroups​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroups() → {[string]: SoundGroup }-- Read-only map of sound groups Returns all sound groups. Read-only table; the instances themselves remain mutable.

searchDocs.title: CreateSound​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.CreateSound( soundDefinition: SoundDefinition-- Serialized sound data to materialize ) → Sound -- Newly created sound instance Converts serialized sound data into a playable Sound instance.

searchDocs.title: FindSoundByName​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> VluxySF.FindSoundByName( name: string,-- Name of the sound to find variance: SoundVariance?,-- Optional variance callbacks parent: Instance? -- Optional parent for the created sound ) → Sound? -- Created sound, if found Finds a sound by name and materializes it as an instance.

searchDocs.title: PlaySoundByName​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.PlaySoundByName( name: string,-- Name of the sound to play variance: SoundVariance?,-- Optional variance callbacks parent: Instance? -- Optional parent for playback ) → () Plays a sound by name and destroys it after playback starts.

searchDocs.title: Variance​
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.Variance( minSpeed: number?,-- Minimum playback speed multiplier (default: 0.95) maxSpeed: number?,-- Maximum playback speed multiplier (default: 1.05) minVolume: number?,-- Minimum volume multiplier (default: 0.95) maxVolume: number?-- Maximum volume multiplier (default: 1.05) ) → SoundVariance-- Randomized variance callbacks Creates variance callbacks for sound playback speed and volume.
